TextENGNew: I was on my way home.
......The cries of the evening cicadas deepened the loneliness of the fading afternoon.
changeReason: New translation renders 'higurashi' as 'evening cicadas,' providing the English meaning where old kept the Japanese term untranslated

TextENGNew: ...It seemed more time had passed than I realized.
The orange of the evening sky was being slowly driven back by the deep indigo advancing from the east.
Exhausted from the gentle, endless uphill slope leading to Hinamizawa... at some point I had dismounted my bicycle and was walking alongside it.
Nothing made any sense...
...Nobody had confronted me face-to-face and said, 'What you did was wrong!'
...No... if anything, that might have actually been easier to bear.
What happened yesterday... truly was nothing more than idle curiosity.
...It was really just an innocent prank... like crossing against a red light because everyone else was doing it too.
The ritual storehouse--sacred ground that only the priest's family was permitted to enter.
Inside, there had certainly been terrifying things beyond anything I could have imagined.
...But thinking about it now, no matter what was stored in there, it didn't justify what I had done.
...Mion seemed to know... what the four of us had done.
...And for some reason, even the police were trying to find out what the four of us had done.
Had we really done... something that terrible......?
Of course we had.
...There are no degrees of wrongdoing.
...I did something wrong.@k That alone was the truth.
changeReason: Old adds 'and yet...' implying unresolved qualification; Japanese says 'That alone was the truth.'

TextENGNew: And yet I couldn't even admit it--I couldn't apologize to Mion or Ooishi-san, only hiding behind vague deflections...
I did something wrong yesterday.
...I trespassed in the forbidden ritual storehouse, on nothing more than a whim.
I'll forget everything I saw inside.
And I'll never do anything like this again.
...I'm sorry. I'm sorry. I'm sorry.
If I apologized like that... would they forgive me...?
The evening air, suddenly turning cold, left no room for such sentiment--it chilled my feet mercilessly, as if urging me to get home as quickly as possible...
...But in the end, the evening air was right.
No matter how many regrets I piled up while trudging along this lonely road, nothing would change.
Time to go home.
I'd go home, retreat to my room, crawl under the covers, and put on some of my favorite music.
...Something so seemingly mundane would probably feel like the greatest comfort in the world today.
changeReason: Old says 'the strongest I've felt all day' (personal energy); new says 'the greatest comfort in the world' (soothing quality). JPN 一番に感じる means 'feel as the best thing,' new captures the comfort nuance better

TextENGNew: Once I'd made up my mind, there was no point dawdling around here.@k I swung my leg back over the bicycle.
I bore down hard on the pedals.
I pumped them faster and faster.
My speed climbed steadily.
The wind cutting against me was mercilessly cold, but it almost felt good--as though it were a small punishment I deserved...
I barely touched my dinner before excusing myself early and retreating to my room.
Mom's voice called up from downstairs.
``Keiichi~@k@vS02/00/132700018. You have a phone call!@k@vS02/00/132700019. It's from Sonozaki-san!``
Sonozaki?@k Mion?
...No. It was Shion.
...That's right, I remembered now.
Shion had wanted to meet today because she had something to tell me.
Ooishi-san had barged in, so she never got the chance.@k ...She had said she would call tonight...
``Hey there.@k@vS02/06/130600204. Quite an ordeal today, wasn't it?``
She said it so casually that it irritated me.@k ...She was the one who ditched me and ran.
``Please don't say that.@k@vS02/06/130600206. I gave you the signal to escape with me, didn't I?@k@vS02/06/130600207. But you just sat there spacing out, Kei-chan.``
``I-I wasn't spacing out!@k@vS02/01/130100350. ...You left me behind in a flash, and after that, I was stuck dealing with that Ooishi guy for ages!``
``Don't come complaining to me about it.``
...H-How can she just brush it off like that?!
She looks so much like Mion, and yet the person inside couldn't be more different.
...I felt a twinge of exasperation at myself when I realized that somewhere in my mind, I'd been expecting the same reaction Mion would give--even though I knew she was a different person entirely.
``...Ah... sorry about that.@k@vS02/01/130100352. Next time I'll be the first one out the door--even if it means leaving you behind.``
``Ladies first, if you please. Ahahahahaha!``
Shion let out a bright, cheerful laugh.
...Hearing her voice like that, I finally realized she hadn't actually been that upset.
...She was right, when I thought about it.
...The root of my foul mood was my own failure to get out of there when I had the chance.
...I should stop blaming others.@k Blaming others was...
``More importantly... you had something you wanted to tell me, right?``
``Oh, yes.@k@vS02/06/130600211. .........If you already know, then it's not really something I need to bring up again.@k@vS02/06/130600212. ...Um... ...do you already know?``
Shion's voice dropped to a sudden whisper, as if checking whether anyone was listening.
...She asked if I 'already knew,' but I had absolutely no idea what she meant.
``...Sorry, but I have no clue what you're talking about.@k@vS02/01/130100355. At least give me a hint.``
``......Which means... ...you don't know, then.``
My clueless question had apparently served as her answer.
But there was no trace of disappointment or humor in Shion's voice.
``Actually... it's about last night, during the Watanagashi Festival.``
A terrible chill shot through my entire body...
...I-Is Shion... going to ask the same thing too...?!
``Hello?@k@vS02/06/130600216. Kei-chan, are you still there...?``
``...Oh... yeah.@k@vS02/01/130100357. I'm listening.@k@vS02/01/130100358. ...What about last night?``
``The four of us... we went in there, right?``
...I didn't respond.
changeReason: Old says 'She didn't beat around the bush' (Shion being direct) but Japanese means 'I didn't respond' (narrator's silence).

TextENGNew: Whether I admitted it or not... it was an absolute fact that couldn't be undone...

``After that, we all walked together... and then split up near the stone steps, right?@k@vS02/06/130600219. Takano-san and Tomitake-san went down to the stream, and you stayed behind, Kei-chan.@k@vS02/06/130600220. And I went off to find my relatives.``
``...Yeah.@k@vS02/01/130100360. What about it?``
``......We won't get anywhere if we keep trading questions back and forth... so let me go first.@k@vS02/06/130600222. All right?``
``You want to know if I saw Tomitake-san and Takano-san last night... right?``
``......Huh?@k@vS02/06/130600224. ...Oh, yes.@k@vS02/06/130600225. ...Did you see the two of them after we parted ways?``
...I'd be lying if I said I wasn't surprised.
...This was the same question I'd been asked three separate times today.
Why?@k Why?@k What the hell was going on?!
``W-Why?!@k@vS02/01/130100363. Why does everyone keep asking me that?!``
``Now, now, Kei-chan!@k@vS02/06/130600227. I'm supposed to be the one asking the questions here.@k@vS02/06/130600228. Please answer me.``
``D-Did you see them, Shion?!@k@vS02/01/130100365. If you answer first, then I'll answer too...!``
It was a pitiful reply--the shabby response of someone who had been thoroughly frightened by everything that happened today.
Shion said nothing for a while.
...Just as I was starting to worry that my response had been a terrible mistake, she finally spoke.
``Of course I didn't.@k@vS02/06/130600230. The relatives I was with can vouch for me.``
``......I didn't see them either.@k@vS02/01/130100367. As for proof, Mion, Rena--everyone was with me. They can all confirm it.``
Once we confirmed our answers matched, we both let out what could only be called sighs of relief...
``Thank you for being honest with me.@k@vS02/06/130600232. ...All right, then... I'll tell you.``
...Apparently, last night, Takano-san and Tomitake-san died.``
``......Sorry, Shion.@k@vS02/01/130100369. ...What did you just say?``
``Last night, Takano-san and Tomitake-san died.``

``Takano-san was found burned to death.@k@vS02/06/130600235. And Tomitake-san... it looked like suicide.@k@vS02/06/130600236. ...I found out this morning.@k@vS02/06/130600237. ...I overheard my dad talking about it on the phone with a relative...``
Late last night.
A police car returning from festival security duty had found Tomitake-san collapsed on the road to Okinomiya.
He had... clawed his own throat open,@k@|@y and died.
``W-What do you mean he clawed his throat open...?@k@vS02/01/130100371. Th-That cheerful, easygoing Tomitake-san committed suicide?!@k@vS02/01/130100372. That has to be a mistake!``

``The police did a proper autopsy!@k@vS02/06/130600239. He did it with his own fingernails!@k@vS02/06/130600240. He scraped and tore at his throat until he ripped open a blood vessel and bled everywhere!@k@vS02/06/130600241. They said he drowned in his own blood!!``
``I've never heard of anyone killing themselves like that!!@k@vS02/01/130100374. You can't just claw your own throat open... that's insane!!``
I realized I'd been yelling too loudly and forced myself to lower my voice...
I didn't want my parents to hear, so I switched to the cordless phone and hurried up to my room.
``...Sorry for shouting.@k@vS02/01/130100376. ...So?@k@vS02/01/130100377. Did Takano-san... was hers a suicide too?``
``Takano-san's case is... a bit unclear......``
Takano-san's body had been found in the mountains of the neighboring prefecture.
changeReason: Old specifies 'Gifu Prefecture'; new says 'the neighboring prefecture.' JPN 隣の県 means 'the neighboring prefecture' without naming it; old added specificity not in the source

TextENGNew: Employees at a nearby rest area had spotted flames in the mountains late at night and called it in.
The firefighters who responded were the ones to discover her.
The autopsy determined it was the burned remains of a half-naked woman.
Identification seemed like it would be difficult, but shortly after, the prefectural police in Okinomiya requested a dental records comparison. The match confirmed the body as Miyo Takano.
The cause of death had not yet been determined, but given that she was found in her undergarments with no clothing nearby, homicide was strongly suspected.
``...They found a kerosene can nearby, so self-immolation hasn't been ruled out either.``
changeReason: Old says 'oil drum' but 灯油缶 means 'kerosene can.'

TextENGNew: ``I-It couldn't have been suicide!!@k@vS02/01/130100379. If someone wanted to die, there are countless easier ways!@k@vS02/01/130100380. There's... no way this was a suicide!!``
``That's what I think too!@k@vS02/06/130600245. There's no way Tomitake-san would kill himself either!``
We had both been talking over each other, and now a breathless silence fell between us.
...As the rush of emotion subsided...@k the true meaning of what Shion had just told me began to sink in...
``...I think they'll call it Oyashiro-sama's curse.@k@vS02/06/130600247. After all, we more than earned that distinction.``
``D-Deserve...? Y-You can't be serious!!@k@vS02/01/130100382. It's not like I...!!``
Shion was the one who suggested we go look! I only went along because she asked!!@k ...I was about to say that, but I stopped myself.
...Blaming someone else wouldn't solve anything.
...No matter how much the others had encouraged me... I could have firmly refused.
It was my own halfhearted curiosity that made me agree to go inside...... That was no one's fault but my own...
``......Shion.@k@vS02/01/130100384. ...When we snuck into the ritual storehouse last night, did you have any idea this might happen...?``
``...Of course not.@k@vS02/06/130600249. Sure, I knew it was risky... but I never dreamed anything like this could happen.``
Those were probably Shion's honest feelings.
...She knew getting caught would mean trouble, but the thrill was part of the adventure.@k ...I felt exactly the same way.
``Never mind that, Kei-chan.@k@vS02/06/130600251. ...Think carefully about this for a moment.``
``...Don't you think this year's curse of Oyashiro-sama is strange?``
...Given how Tomitake-san and Takano-san died, the whole thing was already plenty strange.
With all that in mind, what could she possibly mean by 'strange'...?

``Two bodies were found.@k@vS02/06/130600254. ...Remember what I told you?@k@vS02/06/130600255. Every year, one person dies and one person vanishes.@k@vS02/06/130600256. ...This is the first year both victims have been found dead.``
``...I suppose that's true... but is it really that significant?``

``It is, Kei-chan.@k@vS02/06/130600258. ...Think about this more seriously.@k@vS02/06/130600259. Listen.@k@vS02/06/130600260. If two deaths are being attributed to Oyashiro-sama's curse,@k@vS02/06/130600261. then two sacrifices should also be needed to appease Oyashiro-sama's anger.``

``...Is that really how it works...?@k@vS02/01/130100387. Then what are you saying?@k@vS02/01/130100388. That because Tomitake-san and Takano-san died, two people are going to disappear this year...?``
...I didn't understand why, but...
...even as the words left my mouth, I felt my spine turn to ice.
``...I don't think anyone has gone missing yet.@k@vS02/06/130600263. ...I think the two who will disappear... haven't disappeared yet...``
Shion, like me... must have felt the same chill creeping up her spine.
``Two people... are still going to... disappear......?``
Shion didn't answer.
I couldn't bring myself to say anything more either.
...I could feel the cold seeping through the gaps in my clothing, its icy fingers groping across my chest, reaching to close around my heart...
Still, neither of us could speak.
Tomitake-san, who had opened the lock, was dead. Takano-san, who had exposed the contents of the ritual storehouse, was dead.
...If two more people were to be sacrificed...
Then it would be... none other than us... the ones who had entered the storehouse alongside them...
I wanted so badly for this to be a joke, even now.@k ...But that was a hopelessly selfish wish.
If Tomitake-san and Takano-san had died in some ordinary way--a car accident, for instance--I could have written it off as coincidence.
...But... clawing open your own throat... being doused in kerosene and burned alive in the mountains...@k ...Lined up together, deaths this abnormal couldn't possibly be dismissed as coincidence.
changeReason: Old says 'drowned in oil' but Japanese 灯油を浴びせられて means 'had kerosene poured on her.'

TextENGNew: It was as though... in retribution for sneaking into the ritual storehouse,@k some cruel will was making an example of us.
...Even so... I didn't want to accept this terrifying reality.
...Because accepting it would mean acknowledging that a terrible danger was closing in on Shion and me...
...So I argued against it.@k ...I argued, knowing full well I was running away.

``F-For starters... it's not even in the news!@k@vS02/01/130100394. Something this bizarre would definitely be all over the newspapers and television!@k@vS02/01/130100395. Magazine reporters would come swarming in, thinking it was a great story...!!``
``Of course the media isn't covering it.@k@vS02/06/130600265. ...It was the same way in the past.``
By the time the mysterious deaths had continued for three years running, some tabloid magazines had caught wind of the story and run sensationalized pieces about Hinamizawa.
Fearing the damage to Hinamizawa's reputation, the community and police had conferred.
...Incredibly, starting last year, a policy had been put in place to handle incidents in secret, with no information leaked to the media...
``I hear the village chief and some Sonozaki family big shots put enormous pressure on the police.``
``So you're saying... these deaths... are being covered up in total secrecy?!``

``Exactly.@k@vS02/06/130600268. ...Of course, the police are investigating.@k@vS02/06/130600269. But since it's a covert investigation, I hear they've been placed under severe restrictions.@k@vS02/06/130600270. You could essentially call it obstruction of justice.``
...It was an unbelievable story.
...People had died... and yet it wasn't made public, quietly swept under the rug...
``To put it more bluntly:@k@vS02/06/130600272. no matter who dies on the night of Watanagashi, it will never be made public.``
``...Th-That's absolutely insane...!``
changeReason: Old uses 'biggest pile of bullshit' (vulgar); new uses 'absolutely insane' (neutral). JPN 馬鹿な話 is closer to 'absurd/insane' -- register significantly different

TextENGNew: ``Kei-chan.@k@vS02/06/130600274. ...I told you before that Oyashiro-sama's curse might be the work of the villagers, remember?@k@vS02/06/130600275. ...That's exactly what I mean.``
``...Here in Hinamizawa, at some point, a foundation was laid... where every year, someone can be killed on the night of Watanagashi and it will simply be attributed to Oyashiro-sama's curse.``
changeReason: Old says 'a tradition was created'; new says 'a foundation was laid.' JPN 土壌が作り上げられている uses the metaphor of 'soil/ground being cultivated,' which new captures more accurately

TextENGNew: When someone dies on the night of Watanagashi... it becomes the curse of Oyashiro-sama.
The curse becomes part of the serial mysterious deaths.
...And to prevent Hinamizawa's image from being tarnished further... those incidents are quietly handled behind closed doors...
``That's absurd!!@k@vS02/01/130100399. People die and go missing every year!@k@vS02/01/130100400. No matter how bad the stigma, there has to be a limit to what the police will cover up!``
``...If the annual incidents were obviously suspicious, the police might be able to throw their full weight behind it, but...``

``The thing is, each year's incident gets resolved on its own.@k@vS02/06/130600279. ...It never takes the shape of a 'serial case.'@k@vS02/06/130600280. It just so happens that something unfortunate occurs on the night of Watanagashi every year.@k@vS02/06/130600281. ...That's all it amounts to.``
The perpetrators of the first murder were identified, and most of them were arrested.
The last one remained a fugitive... but the full picture of the case had been established, and it was, for all intents and purposes, resolved.
The accidental death of the pro-dam couple in the second year was ruled a genuine accident.
...Multiple possibilities of homicide were explored, but nothing was found.@k It was settled as an accident.@k Resolved.
In the third year, the shrine priest died of illness.
Hospital doctors had been present at his deathbed, and there were reliable medical records.
They had even performed an autopsy.
The conclusion: not a homicide.@k ...That too was resolved.
In the fourth year, the wife of the pro-dam couple's younger brother was murdered.
changeReason: Old says 'the sister-in-law of the dam-supporting couple'; new says 'the wife of the pro-dam couple's younger brother.' JPN 弟夫婦の妻 means specifically the brother's wife; new is more precise about the family relationship

TextENGNew: The killer was a disturbed man with a criminal record.
changeReason: Old says 'regular psychostimulant usage' but 犯罪の前科 means 'criminal record/prior convictions.'

TextENGNew: He confessed that he found Hinamizawa's curse so fascinating he decided to reenact it.
The killer died in a prison accident, so this case was essentially resolved as well.
...That's right.
Every incident was unrelated to the others, and every one had been individually resolved...
``And yet, somehow, an unfortunate 'coincidence' occurs every year on the night of Watanagashi.@k@vS02/06/130600283. ...Even if you wanted to call it a serial case, the pieces don't connect.``
...And yet, every single year, someone always dies, and someone always vanishes!``
Takano-san had told me this story the day before the festival, during the preparations... but now that this year's 'curse' had actually happened, I couldn't simply laugh it off.

``......Then... does that mean... we're being targeted too...?@k@vS02/01/130100402. Just like how one person dies and one disappears each year... are you saying we'll be dragged along with Tomitake-san and Takano-san... and made to vanish too...?!``
...My lips had gone completely dry at some point... and just getting those words out was... agonizing.
Shion didn't answer.
...But her silence was the clearest affirmation she could have given.

``...We... sure, maybe we went somewhere we weren't supposed to...@k@vS02/01/130100404. ...but all we did was look, right?@k@vS02/01/130100405. We didn't steal anything, didn't take anything out... we didn't tell anyone what we saw, right...?``
I already felt more than enough regret for what I'd done...
But still... was what we did really so terrible... that Tomitake-san and Takano-san deserved to die such gruesome deaths?!
``...Besides, I didn't even care about any of what I saw in there!!@k@vS02/01/130100407. Takano-san was the only one getting all excited about it!@k@vS02/01/130100408. She's the only one at fault!!``
``I have nothing to do with any of this!!@k@vS02/01/130100410. I'm not interested! I'm not involved! I couldn't care less about what was in there!!!``
Even as I said it, I knew it wouldn't solve anything.
Deep down, my calmer self looked on in disgust at this pointless howling.@k ...But... my emotions had burst through the dam, and I couldn't hold them back anymore.

``I never had any interest in that place from the start!!!@k@vS02/01/130100412. All I wanted was to watch Rika-chan's performance!!@k@vS02/01/130100413. You're the one who dragged me out of there, Shion!``
I don't care what those two were up to--stealing, sneaking around, whatever!!!@k@vS02/01/130100415. That's right! You're the one who suggested we go inside, Shion!!``
You said all that stuff like 'Kei-chan, you really should see this,' and you pulled me in there!!!@k@vS02/01/130100417. What are you going to do about this?!?!``

``I had nothing to do with this!!!@k@vS02/01/130100419. What are you going to do?@k@vS02/01/130100420. What the hell are you going to do?!@k@vS02/01/130100421. How are you going to take responsibility for this?!?!@k@vS02/01/130100422. Huh?!@k@vS02/01/130100423. Hey, are you even listen......... aagh!``
*Click.*
Without any warning, the line went dead--leaving behind only the faint sound of a receiver being slammed down.
...Shion must have been just as terrified as I was...... and I had said such horribly one-sided things to her...
The frenzy of emotion vanished as if it had never existed, replaced by a surging tide of regret for the irresponsible things I had blurted out in the heat of the moment.
I could call Shion's name into the dead receiver all I wanted... but it was far too late.
.........What have I... done?
...All Shion had been trying to do was warn me about 'something' that was about to come down on us...
...And I... had done nothing but whine like a spoiled child...
I was about to call her back when I realized she lived in Okinomiya.
...I didn't know her phone number.
.........Having declared to Mion that I hadn't seen Shion last night, I couldn't possibly call Mion now and ask for Shion's number... I was far too afraid.
...I couldn't even call her back...!
I was crushed under the weight of hopeless regret...
......I set the receiver back in its cradle and simply prayed... that Shion would call me one more time.
Please, Shion......
......Forgive me... and call again......!
Riiiiing...
``H-Hello, this is Maebara!!``
I snatched up the receiver as though missing this exact moment would mean the call was lost forever.

``Excuse me, is this the Maebara residence?@k@vS02/36/132900002. I'm sorry for calling so late...@k@vS02/36/132900003. My name is Kimiyoshi.@k@vS02/36/132900004. May I speak with the head of the household?``
It was the voice of a girl--clearly not Shion.@k ...All my hope drained away in an instant.
``No, that's not me.@k@vS02/01/130100426. ...If you need my dad, I'll go get him.``
I was about to set the receiver down when the caller stopped me in a flustered voice.
``Oh, no no, that's quite all right if he's busy.@k@vS02/36/132900006. ...I'm sorry to trouble you, but... has my grandfather happened to stop by your home?``
``...No, nobody's come by.``
``I see!@k@vS02/36/132900008. I'm so sorry for disturbing you this late.@k@vS02/36/132900009. Goodbye......``

``Excuse me, is this the Maebara residence?@k@vS02/00/kimi0300002. I'm sorry for calling so late...@k@vS02/00/kimi0300003. My name is Kimiyoshi.@k@vS02/00/kimi0300004. May I speak with the head of the household?``
It was the voice of someone older--clearly not Shion.@k ...All my hope drained away in an instant.
changeReason: Old says 'voice of a girl' but 年配の人 means 'older/elderly person' -- opposite descriptor.

TextENGNew: ``No, that's not me.@k@vS02/01/130100426. ...If you need my dad, I'll go get him.``
I was about to set the receiver down when the caller stopped me in a flustered voice.
``Oh, no no, that's quite all right if he's busy.@k@vS02/00/kimi0300006. ...I'm sorry to trouble you, but... has my grandfather happened to stop by your home?``
``...No, nobody's come by.``
``I see!@k@vS02/00/kimi0300008. I'm so sorry for disturbing you this late.@k@vS02/00/kimi0300009. Goodbye......``
*Clack.*
...I had no idea who that was, but what if Shion had been trying to call while the line was busy?! ...I seethed with that kind of self-centered anger.
Hey, hey......@k Get a grip, Keiichi Maebara.
...These self-centered outbursts were exactly what made Shion angry at me in the first place...

...And yet, the more I thought about it, the more I convinced myself that during those few short minutes, Shion had tried to call,@k heard the busy signal, lost heart, and given up on reaching me entirely...
Calm down, Keiichi Maebara...
...If Shion calls again, start by apologizing in a calm voice...
...If I can do that, Shion should understand...
But... no matter how long I waited...
...the phone never rang again that night...
